mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above nine percent indicates a deeply slack labor market near the cyclical trough; in this threshold state, even marginal improvements in labor market sentiment reduce recession risk and generate sharp positive S&P 500 returns as discount rates fall.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or the mean forward return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below one percent and industrial production is growing month-over-month, the interaction of extreme monetary accommodation and recovering real output produces strongly positive S&P 500 returns because liquidity injections coincide with improving fundamentals.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce higher mean 20-day returns than either single-series signal alone, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05) in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in housing starts predicts positive S&P 500 returns specifically in post-crisis recovery regimes because construction activity drives broad multiplier effects through employment and materials demand that are larger than in normal expansions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 during post-crisis recovery periods, or the mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05) in those regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation above two percent year-over-year when unemployment exceeds six percent generates negative real wage growth and compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A positive three-month change in nonfarm payrolls signals improving labor income and household spending power, which drives consumption and lifts S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate over a three-month window increases the cost of capital and reduces the present value of future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales captures real-time consumer spending momentum, which directly translates into corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
